mechanism_narrative: Sustained low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of elevated household savings rates and accelerating housing starts channels excess liquidity into consumer durables and real estate sectors, driving broad market earnings growth and positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression of 60-day forward returns, or the combined signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark over 3 consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly boosts corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month during supply-driven growth phases.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >50% of periods when INDPRO increases month-over-month, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 260, persistent inflation erodes real corporate earnings and forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in >65% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 260, or the inflation threshold fails to predict return direction with a t-statistic magnitude below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a broad-based labor market tightening, which supports consumer spending forecasts and drives positive short-term S&P 500 momentum.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 5-day forward returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in a walk-forward validation framework.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, which mechanically translates into higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in >50% of periods following positive GDPC1 revisions, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 5-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero policy rates and rapidly rising consumer prices creates negative real yields and forces equity markets to discount future earnings growth, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month during stagflationary risk regimes.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% and CPIAUCSL rises >0.5% month-over-month, or the interaction term loses predictive significance in out-of-sample tests.
